Public Works Commission Meeting 10-27-21 Item 4.A.

Public Works Commission Agenda Report

To: Chair Simmens and Members of the Public Works Commission Prepared by: Troy Spayd, Assistant Public Works Director/City Engineer Approved by: Rob DuBoux, Public Works Director/City Engineer Date prepared:   October 21, 2021 Meeting date: October 27, 2021 Subject: Capital Improvement Projects and Disaster Recovery Projects Status Report

RECOMMENDED ACTION: Receive and file report on the status of the City’s current and upcoming Capital Improvements Projects and Disaster Recovery Projects. DISCUSSION:  Staff will provide a status update on the following active projects in the Fiscal Year 2021-2022 Capital Improvement Program:

Capital Improvement Projects (Design)

Marie Canyon Green Streets

¾ Updates:  The City has received Measure W funds to complete this project.

Staff is working on the project plans to prepare for public bidding. ¾

Project Schedule:

 Complete Design: December 2021  Begin Construction: Winter 2021

PCH Median Improvements Project

PCH Median Improvements Project ¾ Updates:

  Consultant is working on the final design changes. Staff received the project’s CDP from the Planning Commission.  Staff is coordinating with Caltrans for the final project approvals.

Interviews were conducted with qualified consultants to provide construction management services this

month and staff is in the final stages of selecting a firm. ¾ Project Schedule:

  Complete Design: Fall 2021  Begin Construction: Winter 2021/2022

  The  project  is  scheduled  for  Planning Commission hearing on November 15, 2021.

  Staff is working out the final details of the project with the County  and  will  be  finalizing  the  design.    The  approved  CDP  was appealed and staff is preparing to respond to the appeal.

Permanent Skate Park

¾ Project Description:

  This project includes the designing and constructing a permanent skate park located on the Crummer/Case Court parcel adjacent to Malibu Bluffs Park.  The project would include parking and additional site amenities such as trash cans, benches, tables, and restrooms.

¾

PCH at Trancas Canyon Road Right Turn Lane ¾ Project Description:

  A  request  for  proposals  from  qualified  firms  was  released  on October 7, 2021 with proposals due November 10 th . ¾

Capital Improvement Projects (Construction)

Malibu Park Drainage Improvements

¾ Project  Description:

  This  project  consists  of  storm  drain  repairs  within  the Malibu Park neighborhood.

These repairs are the result of damage caused by the subsequent storms after the Woolsey Fire. Work will include clearing and grubbing, demolition, cold milling, removal of existing corrugated metal pipe, new asphalt berms/dikes, pavement repairs, pavement overlay, striping, new reinforced concrete pipes, and rock slope protection.

¾ Updates: Construction continues at the Morning View location.

The new 60" HDPE pipe has been installed and the contractor continues to construct the new  basins  and  backfill  around  the  new  headwall.  The  contractor  will  also begin working at the other locations per the project plans and specifications. Work will include temporary lane closures with flaggers.

¾ Project Schedule: August 2021 through October 2021

Page 3 of 7
Agenda Item # 4.A.

2021 Annual Street Maintenance

¾ Project  Description:

    This  project  provides  for  the  reconstruction  and maintenance  of  City  streets.  Including  asphalt  pavement  repairs,  Type  II slurry seal, speed humps and new thermoplastic striping.

¾ Updates:

  The  contractor  has  mobilized  and  continues  to  perform  the necessary  work  per  the  contract  plans  and  specifications.

To  date  all  the pavement repairs, crack seal work and new speed humps on Birdview Ave have  been  completed.  Crews  will  continue  removing  existing  striping  and pavement markers in preparation for next weeks scheduled slurry seal work.

¾ Project Schedule: August 2021 through October 2021

Capital Improvement Projects (Not Started)

Vehicle Protection Devices

¾ Project Description:  Per Malibu Municipal Code Section 17.48.

070 requires vehicle impact protection devices to be installed for all parking spaces located adjacent  to  any  outdoor  pedestrian  seating  area.    There  are  two  locations within the City’s right-of-way  and City-owned  property that would require a vehicle impact protection device.

Legacy Park Paver Repair Project

¾ Project Description:

  This project will consist of removing and repairing a large section  of  pavers  in  Legacy  Park.

The  pavers  have  become  uneven  and damaged in several areas

Malibu Bluffs Park South Walkway

¾ Project Description:

  This project will include replacing the existing sidewalk located on the south side of Malibu Bluffs Park.

Trancas Playground Resurfacing

Trancas Playground Resurfacing ¾ Project Description:

  The project will include removing and replacing the upper layer of the playground surfacing at Trancas Canyon Park.

PCH Overhead Warning Signs

¾ Project Description:

  This project was identified in the PCH Safety Study and includes  the  installation  of  overhead  warning  signs  on  PCH  near Moonshadows restaurant and Big Rock Drive.

Capital Improvement Projects (Completed)

Civic Center Way Improvements

¾ Updates:

  The  contractor  has  completed  the  planting  material  maintenance period  as  described  within  the  project  documents.

Council  accepted  this project as complete at the September 13th City Council Meeting.Page 4 of 7

Outdoor Warning Sirens (FEMA Project)

¾ Updates:

  This project will be funded through a grant from FEMA after the Woolsey  Fire.  Staff  received  proposals  from  consultants  to  design  and  to develop a plan that includes the evaluation of a siren system and possible locations.

It is anticipated a consultant will be hired in November 2021 and schedule a kick-off meeting in early December 2021.Page 5 of 7
Agenda Item # 4.A.

¾ Project Schedule:  Begin Design: Winter 2021

Storm Drain Master Plan (FEMA Project)

¾ Project Description:

  This project will be funded through a grant from FEMA after the Woolsey Fire.

The City will create a complete inventory of storm drains,  culverts,  debris  basins,  manholes,  and  other  drainage  structures within the City.

¾ Updates:

    Proposals  were  received  in  September  2021  and  are  currently under review.  Staff anticipates completing the selection process next month and schedule a kick-off meeting in early December 2021.

¾ Estimated Schedule:  Completion Date:  Spring 2022

Disaster Projects (Construction) None at this time

Disaster Projects (Completed) Guardrail Replacement Citywide (FEMA Project) ¾ Project Description: This project consisted of replacing the damaged guardrail throughout the City as a result of the Woolsey Fire.

Corral Canyon Road Bridge Repairs (FEMA Project)

¾ Project Description:

Corral Canyon Culvert Repairs (FEMA Project)

¾ Project Description:  This project has been cancelled as it could not get FEMA approval.

Disaster Projects (Not Started)

Clover Heights Storm Drain (FEMA Project)

¾ Project Description:

  This project consists of design and construction of a few storm drains on Clover Heights.  The existing storm drain facility ends at the intersection  of  Clover  Heights  and  Harvester  Road.    During  storms,  this intersection floods and causes debris to block the road.  An extended storm drain towards the end of Clover Heights will help eliminate this issue.

¾

Estimated Schedule:  Complete Design: Spring 2022  Begin Construction:

 Summer 2022
